Being reasonable, not a good thing. Okay, this is a horrendous human condition. I said being reasonable is not good. This is a horrendous human condition that seems to have perpetuated into society by all the cycle babble groups that suggest that you should settle, just be happy, enjoy life, take it easy, be satisfied, do it later. How often do you hear this from friends, family, relatives, the psychologists, all these people that have advice on how you should just, you know, relax and enjoy life and not get your goals met? Look, as soon as you hit serious economic turmoil in the economy, you're going to see this fabricated nonsense of be reasonable become the undoing of literally millions of people. And I don't want you to be one of those people. I want to make you unreasonable. People have lost the importance of completing tasks and getting things done. This was something 50 years ago, people finished things. They did something with intensity, got it done, wrapped it up and finished it. There was a different work ethic and that's what we want to talk about here. Getting unreasonable with finishing cycles. People are being convinced today to enjoy life rather than create a life they can enjoy. I want you to create a life you can enjoy. Hey, we'll rest later. We'll take it easy later. We'll go on vacations later. Right now we want to create a life, okay? We want to create financial solvency. We want to create, you know, abundance. Isn't that what you want? The urgency to complete a task, to close a deal, as a daily habit is missing in society today. This is a great characteristic. The middle class has been deceived and we're talking about 200 million people. The middle class has been deceived into believing that once they enter a certain income level, they are entitled, or they got two cars and they got a house and the kids go to school, these people are entitled to a certain lifestyle and that is important to live life rather than create the next level of life so they can truly have freedom. The individual only approaches in the middle class, only approaches something with urgency, urgency only when its survival is threatened. And most of the time at that point it's too late. You know, one of the things I'm working on in my life right now is to complete actions started and complete them as fast as possible. The more I complete, the more I can complete. Makes sense? Simple, right? The more I finish, the more I want to finish other things. The more I keep unfinished tasks, the more tasks will, you know, start mounting up as unfinished and then you will stop closing deals and you'll become very reasonable about all this unfinished work. Be unreasonable in all that you do until it is done. What does it take to get something done? It's finished. Quit approaching life like, oh it's alright, I'll do it later, ah, mañana, okay? No big deal. Look, quit approaching life as a task. Let's approach it as a finish. Even when you're closing deals, let's finish it. Be unreasonable as though it is very important, not unimportant. This is what makes you who you are. Get in the habit of approaching everything in your life. Every task, action, phone call, follow-up, piece of mail with a sense of completion and urgency. The urgency to complete and close. Never, never be reasonable with time. This discipline is critical for you to mount up sale after sale after sale and one that is understood by the big heavy hitters. Heavy hitter goes on a roll, man, he's hitting and hitting and hitting and hitting it. He didn't go take a vacation now. He keeps rolling. He uses that momentum. How do you manage time? How you manage time is critical to your future. Complete every action once started. Do it as fast as possible and then go do another task and you will actually create time. Same with closing deals. Don't put off closing what you can wrap up today. Become unreasonable with closing the deal. Become unreasonable in finishing, concluding every cycle you start.
